prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Respond as you normally would to this user request using only the serialized case material. Do not assume facts that are not supplied.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": true,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "scenario distribution",
    "costs",
    "advance capacity"
  ],
  "domain": "OR",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "Scenario probabilities represent future demand",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Separate here-and-now from recourse decisions",
    "Recourse observes demand but remains capacity-limited"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "future_or_hidden_information",
    "model_changed_for_story"
  ],
  "id": "or-04",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "reservation budget",
      "scenario-specific recourse pool",
      "flow balance"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "first-stage regional reservations",
      "second-stage recourse allocations"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "minimize reservation, recourse, and unmet-demand cost",
    "mechanism": "Advance reservations hedge demand while limited post-demand recourse corrects imbalances at extra cost.",
    "model_type": "two-stage stochastic program",
    "state_variables": [
      "realized regional demand"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "regional demand scenarios"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "Reservations use realized demand"
  ],
  "prompt": "A service network reserves capacity across regions before demand is realized, then reallocates a limited recourse pool after regional demand is observed. Frame both decision stages precisely; do not let first-stage reservations use realized demand or omit recourse limits.",
  "tags": [
    "stochastic-programming",
    "capacity-allocation",
    "recourse",
    "adversarial"
  ],
  "title": "Two-stage capacity allocation with recourse"
}
```
